content.text: We have POD 32Z430011909019C which is active in contract МПМТ-2406000211 from 01.07.2024 to 31.01.2026 (customer with identifier 4506098796 - status is LOST) and in contract МПОК-2512000711 from 01.02.2026 (customer with identifier 7710028805). The POD is included in a request for disconnection →
For the old customer 4506098796  we have a label “POD is disconnected”
We should not have a label for the old customer.
Disconnection of the power supply - Edit - Phoenix - Confluence
Rule 1 - POD is active in a current contract
in at least one contract, display
Rule 2 - POD is not active in any current contract
not active in any contract at the moment
in which the POD was active.
“Most recent” means the contract with the
of the POD on that contract) among all historical contracts where the POD was active.
We should not mark customers with the label "Pod is disconnected" when the customer status is "LOST".

hello , i can’t found any description about this case so i think it’s looks like change request.
Example with customer UIC 4506098796 and POD
32Z430011909019C on DEV. The POD are deactivated from contract EPES2509041722 and activated in contract EPES2606000005 (UIC 8312201073) from 01.02.2026. Request for disconnection for UIC 8312201075 →
and we still have label “POD is disconnected” in the old customer UIC 4506098796.
We are waiting for logic review from BG side.

The system should display the label DISCONNECTED in the customer which has a contract in which the disconnected POD is currently active. If the POD is not active in any contract at the moment then the label DISCONNECTED should be in the customer which has the latest contract in the past in which the POD was active

Hello, Please, check this example on DEV : We have UIC 203906530 with contract EPES2606001156, which was terminated on 31.05.2026. POD 32Z4143654578888 was moved to a new contract, EPES2606001157, for a different customer UIC 203884969 , which has not yet been activated.
There are overdue liabilities associated with the old contract, and a disconnection for them:
However, the "POD is disconnected" label does not appear for UIC 203906530, but it should. I need to check this with our BA.

We should not mark customers with the label "Pod is disconnected" when the customer status is "LOST" should be removed from the logic. It’s not added from us and in the described case in my previous comment, the label "POD is disconnected" should appear in UIC 203906530.
Hello, yes, this is our mistake, and we will fix the issue. Sorry
